results.actual_model_route: hermes --provider openai-codex -m gpt-5.5 -t file -z <prompt pointing to review-packet.txt>
documentation_gaps: The committed docs retain a stale/confusing note in docs/planning/artifact-manifest.json claiming no_commit_reason="Workspace <local-workspace>/lingotorte is not a git repository", while the review packet records committed git metadata for bcd74c5. This is not a blocker for implementation, but it should be cleaned up to avoid misleading future artifact readers.

Public-doc and OSS evidence tables still contain summarized quotes and license notes without durable retrieval timestamps, source snapshots, package versions, or license-file hashes; the final docs mostly mitigate this by labeling candidates as revalidate-before-adoption, but implementation tasks must not treat those claims as verified adoption/legal conclusions.

Screenshot references remain absolute local paths outside the repository. The safety review correctly treats them as sanitized/local non-distributable evidence, but future readers should not assume those images are part of the durable committed bundle.
suggestions: Update or remove stale no_commit_reason metadata in docs/planning/artifact-manifest.json after the repository is committed.
For P0, create docs/dev/dependency-provenance.md with retrieval date, exact version/commit, license path/hash, and local/offline behavior for each dependency before adoption.
Keep generated scripts clearly marked as local documentation maintenance tools; if they are retained, consider parameterizing the repository root instead of hard-coding <local-workspace>/lingotorte.
When implementation begins, convert the feature playbook rows into bounded cards that each name fixtures, no-network checks, expected files, and open human decisions.

results.summary: PASS. The committed packet is a planning/documentation bundle, not an implementation, and it consistently preserves the local-first, owned-media, no-proprietary-copying, no-DRM, no-private-API/storage/token-dump, no-account-mutation, and online-provider-opt-in boundaries. The final bundle addresses the prior challenge-review blockers with a dedicated safety/privacy boundary review, split mission deliverables, concrete MVP spike gates, no-network/account-mutation patterns, typed architecture/data model guidance, and explicit revalidation requirements for OSS/license/dependency claims. I found no blocking security, privacy, IP, implementability, or secret-exposure issue. The main remaining issues are non-blocking artifact-hygiene/provenance gaps: one stale no_commit_reason in a planning manifest, absolute local screenshot references outside the repo, and public/OSS evidence that must still be revalidated before dependency adoption or legal/license claims.

documentation_gaps: docs/planning/artifact-manifest.json still carries a stale no_commit_reason claiming the workspace is not a git repository, which is now incorrect at commit bcd74c5.

Two artifact manifests coexist (docs/planning/artifact-manifest.json = 7-item parent bundle; docs/final/artifact-manifest.json = 15-item split-path bundle). Their relationship is explained in docs/planning/documentation-index.md but not cross-referenced inside the manifests themselves.
suggestions: Update or prune docs/planning/artifact-manifest.json so its git/commit assumptions match the now-committed state, and clarify the relationship between the two manifests.
Wire validate_final_bundle.py and update_manifest.py into a CI or pre-commit hook so hashes, required-file coverage, feature terms, and local markdown links stay continuously enforced.

In docs/research/live-ui-inventory.md and live-ui-inventory-expanded.md, explicitly tag the disclosed one-time continue-watching side effect as a completed historical inspection so future readers do not treat live CDP inspection as ongoing/sanctioned activity.

results.summary: This is a coherent, safety-conscious planning/documentation bundle for a local-first, privacy-preserving Lingopie-like learning app. The IP/privacy/DRM boundaries are explicit and consistently propagated, evidence is labeled and provenance-disciplined, and the final/spec/architecture/product/review/plan deliverables agree with each other via a documented crosswalk. Implementability is strong: milestones P0-P8 with fixtures, acceptance criteria, dependency/license-provenance gates, and preserved open human decisions. Manifest hashes cross-check, and helper scripts perform only local file I/O. No blocking security, logic, or compatibility issues were found.

results.actual_model_route: hermes --provider minimax-oauth -m MiniMax-M3 -t file -z <prompt pointing to review-packet.txt>
documentation_gaps: docs/spec/lingopie-behavior-reference.md feature-behavior matrix (lines ~259-281) still uses free-text labels ('public docs', 'OSS source/docs', 'inference/recommendation') rather than the normalized bracketed labels (PUBLIC-DOC, OSS-DOC-SOURCE, DESIGN-RECOMMENDATION) defined in docs/planning/evidence-index.md; the doc defines its own label set on lines 7-10 so the inconsistency is contained, but cross-doc normalization would help implementation agents.

Public-doc evidence rows (E-LP-PUB-001..016 in docs/spec/lingopie-behavior-reference.md and docs/research/public-product-cartography.md) cite URLs without retrieval dates; evidence-index.md already downgrades these to revalidate-before-use, but no durable provenance appendix exists yet.

OSS substrate license/version claims (asbplayer MIT, ts-fsrs MIT, subs2srs/Y'ALL MP/LLPlayer GPLv3, WhisperX/stable-ts to be revalidated, Anki NOASSERTION) are reproduced from parent artifacts without retrieval date or current license-file hash; oss-substrate-assessment.md and evidence-index.md explicitly require a per-candidate provenance artifact before adoption, but it has not been created.

docs/planning/evidence-cartography.md still labels AGENTS.md/mission-derived boundaries (E-LOCAL-001..006) as 'inference/recommendation'; the repair lives in the newer docs/planning/evidence-index.md and documentation-index.md crosswalk, but the retained parent artifact is internally inconsistent with the new label taxonomy.
suggestions: Add a small provenance appendix (URL + retrieval date + commit/version + license-file hash) for each E-LP-PUB-* and E-OSS-* row before any implementation card is opened; evidence-index.md already prescribes the artifact shape.

Decide explicitly whether the four local screenshots (<local-home>/lingopie-cdp-*.png) stay referenced as local non-distributable only (current default) or are sanitized and inlined; add a one-line screenshot-policy header in the behavior reference for clarity.

Add a 'why this differs from E-LP-LIVE-*' line for each feature-behavior matrix row in lingopie-behavior-reference.md that already collides with public-doc behavior, so the design-recommendation vs observed mechanics distinction is visible per row.

Consider splitting evidence-cartography.md and adding a thin pointer note that project-constraint rows are retagged PROJECT-CONSTRAINT in evidence-index.md, to reduce the chance that an autonomous implementation agent reads the older table as authoritative.

For the upcoming P0 spike, instantiate the fixture directory and a no-network harness as a sibling planning artifact (even if empty) so the mvp-spike-plan.md 'exact safe inputs' references resolve to real files, not just shapes.

results.summary: The Lingotorte planning bundle at commit bcd74c5c is a coherent, evidence-backed, local-first planning artifact that should not be blocked by this reviewer. Safety/privacy/IP boundaries are explicit and consistent across AGENTS.md, README.md, the mission statement, the mission brief, the safety review, and the planning-side safety doc: no proprietary copying, no DRM/circumvention, read-only/sanitized live UI inspection only, own/local media default, online providers disabled by default with explicit opt-in, Anki export-only by default, screenshots local non-distributable. Evidence-label discipline is repaired by docs/planning/evidence-index.md (PROJECT-CONSTRAINT / MISSION-REQUIREMENT / SANITIZED-LIVE-UI / PUBLIC-DOC / OSS-DOC-SOURCE / DESIGN-RECOMMENDATION / OPEN-DECISION) and the safety review, feature playbook, implementation plan, and architecture docs all use these normalized labels; the only contained drift is in retained parent artifacts (evidence-cartography.md, lingopie-behavior-reference.md matrix) and the documentation-index.md crosswalk explicitly explains the split. The final bundle, implementation plan, feature playbook, architecture, data model, language/SRS plan, MVP spike plan, and reading order agree with each other and with the mission brief. Implementability is well-supported: implementation card template, fixture shapes, no-network/no-account-mutation test pattern, dependency provenance checklist, and open human-decision gates (Polish-first, asbplayer substrate, Anki role, online strictness, Mastered semantics, screenshot policy, backup media policy) are all in place. Generated scripts (generate_final_bundle.py, update_manifest.py, validate_final_bundle.py) are local-only and contain no credentials, no network calls, and no exfiltration risk. No security, logic, or compatibility blockers found; documentation gaps are limited to retrievable improvements (durable provenance appendix for public-doc and OSS substrate evidence, label-table migration of the lingopie-behavior-reference matrix, screenshot-policy resolution) that the bundle itself flags as pre-implementation work.
